core_mechanism: a normalized inner-product (cross-correlation / cosine-similarity) metric on a function space that treats each candidate function as a vector and quantifies how distinguishable a family of them is via pairwise correlations plus an eigenmode (orthogonal-basis) decomposition of that space
brief_reason: The abstract measures the observational independence of models by computing the cross-correlation of their shapes, uses a normalized 'shape correlator' as a fast similarity test, standardizes normalization via the shape autocorrelator, and applies an eigenmode decomposition to characterize model independence.
